intro: Davallodes novoguineensis, also known as New Guinea fern, is a species of fern in the family Davalliaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in New Guinea and other parts of the Pacific, to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and pain, due to its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The bioactive constituents of Davallodes novoguineensis include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic compounds, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues.
